footnote: Scudetti follow the official FIGC record back to 1898, the pre-Serie A championship era included — with 2005 revoked and 2006 reassigned to Inter after Calciopoli. Never-champions count from their first Serie A match; the single national league began in 1929. The 20 clubs shown are the 2026–27 season's.

lastTitle.clinch: won 3–0 at Sassuolo on the final day, Giroud striking twice, to finish two points clear of Inter
beat Parma 3–1 at the Olimpico on the final day — Totti, Montella, Batistuta — needing the win with Juventus two points behind

teams.name: Atalanta
teams.firstGameNote: Held Genoa 0–0 in their 1937 debut; nine decades on, even Gasperini's three straight third places and a 2024 Europa League couldn't deliver La Dea a scudetto.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Inter 2–0 in Italy's only-ever title play-off, at Rome's Olimpico, days after club president Renato Dall'Ara died
beat Bari 2–0 at the Amsicora — a Gigi Riva diving header first — to bring the scudetto to Sardinia, the only island title ever

teams.name: Como 1907
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Palermo 1–0 at the Sinigaglia in their 1949 Serie A debut as the league's only all-Italian squad; 76 years later the Fàbregas era finally topped that season's sixth place — fourth, and a first Champions League.

lastTitle.clinch: won 2–0 at Juventus with a game to spare — Chiarugi and Maraschi silencing Turin

teams.name: Frosinone
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 to Torino at the tiny Stadio Matusa in their 2015 debut; three Serie A visits, three one-season stays, never higher than 18th — and they're back again for 2026–27.

teams.name: Genoa

lastTitle.clinch: drew 1–1 at Savoia in Torre Annunziata to take the national final 4–2 on aggregate — the first championship celebrated with the scudetto patch itself

teams.lineage: Founded 1893 as Genoa Cricket & Athletic Club — Italy's oldest football club; all nine titles by 1924.

teams.name: Inter Milan

lastTitle.clinch: beat Parma 2–0 at San Siro — Thuram and Mkhitaryan — sealing scudetto No. 21 with three games to spare

teams.lineage: Won 1930 as Ambrosiana and 1938/1940 as Ambrosiana-Inter — fascist-era renames of Internazionale.

teams.name: Juventus

lastTitle.clinch: beat Sampdoria 2–0 in an empty COVID-era Allianz Stadium — Ronaldo and Bernardeschi — for a record ninth straight title

teams.lineage: Official count is 36: the 2005 title was revoked and 2006 reassigned after Calciopoli.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Reggina 3–0 while Juventus, delayed by a Perugia downpour, lost 1–0 — a nine-point spring deficit fully overturned

teams.name: US Lecce
teams.firstGameNote: Held reigning champions Verona 2–2 at the Bentegodi in their 1985 debut, then finished bottom anyway; ninth place in 1989 — with a young Antonio Conte in midfield — is still the ceiling.

teams.name: AC Monza
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 to Torino in their 2022 debut — the club's first top-flight match in 110 years, delivered by Berlusconi and Galliani; relegated in 2025, back via the play-offs for 2026–27.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Cagliari 2–0 at the Maradona on the final day — a McTominay bicycle kick — to hold off Inter by a point

teams.name: Parma
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 to Juventus at the Tardini in their 1990 debut; the Parmalat years brought eight trophies, two UEFA Cups and a 1997 near-miss — every prize but the one this clock counts.

teams.name: Sassuolo
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–0 at Torino in their 2013 debut; months later teenager Domenico Berardi put four past AC Milan — a town of 40,000 still dreaming bigger than sixth.

lastTitle.clinch: drew 1–1 with Cesena on the final day as Juventus fell at Perugia — Toro's first title since the Superga air disaster took the Grande Torino

teams.name: Udinese
teams.firstGameNote: Hammered 6–2 at San Siro by Milan's Gre-No-Li trio in their 1950 debut; their best season ever, second place in 1955, ended with a tribunal relegating them over a two-year-old bribery case.

teams.name: Venezia
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 0–0 at Liguria in their 1939 debut with a young Valentino Mazzola aboard — sold to Torino three years later for a record fee to become the heart of the Grande Torino.
